chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يَجُوزُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ قَتْلُهُ وَ مَا يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 97 – What is allowed for the one in Ihraam to kill, and what expiation is Obligated upon him with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/327)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/327)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يَقْتُلُ الْبَقَّةَ وَ الْبُرْغُوثَ إِذَا أَرَادَاهُ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Musna Bin Abdul Salaam, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from one of the two (5th or 6th Imamasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the one in Ihraam (whether he can) kill the bug and the flea, when they intend (to attack) him’. Heasws said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Muthanna’ ibn ‘Abd al-Salam from Zurarah who has said the following: “I once asked one of the two Imam, (abu Ja’far or abu ‘Abd Allah), ‘Alayhim al-Salam, ‘Can one in the state of Ihram kill a bug or flea if it attacks him?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he can do so.’”
